visual_elements.text: Comparing Expected Value and Expected Utility
visual_elements.role_tier_reason: Large top heading labels the slide topic.

visual_elements.text: Expected Utility • A subjective measure • Reflects an individual's personal satisfaction and risk tolerance • Allows individuals to make decisions that align with their personal risk aversion and preferences • Can vary significantly from person to person • Incorporates personal judgments and feelings about risk and reward
visual_elements.role_tier_reason: Main comparison text panel used for reading and confirming points.

visual_elements.text: Expected Value • An objective measure • Remains constant regardless of who is evaluating it • Calculated based on probabilities and outcomes • Provides a consistent metric • Does not change with individual perceptions or preferences
visual_elements.role_tier_reason: Main comparison text panel used for reading and confirming points.

visual_elements.text: In summary, while expected value offers a stable, objective assessment, expected utility provides a personalized approach, incorporating individual risk preferences and satisfaction levels. Now, how can leaders effectively deliver value in public health?
visual_elements.role_tier_reason: Bottom concluding callout summarizes the comparison and transition question.

perception_artifact.extracted_text: Comparing Expected Value and Expected Utility Expected Utility • A subjective measure • Reflects an individual's personal satisfaction and risk tolerance • Allows individuals to make decisions that align with their personal risk aversion and preferences • Can vary significantly from person to person • Incorporates personal judgments and feelings about risk and reward Expected Value • An objective measure • Remains constant regardless of who is evaluating it • Calculated based on probabilities and outcomes • Provides a consistent metric • Does not change with individual perceptions or preferences In summary, while expected value offers a stable, objective assessment, expected utility provides a personalized approach, incorporating individual risk preferences and satisfaction levels. Now, how can leaders effectively deliver value in public health?
perception_artifact.layout_description: A large dark-blue title spans the top. Two pale green rounded rectangles sit side by side in the middle with comparison bullet lists, and a blue summary paragraph appears along the bottom left.
perception_artifact.slide_title: Comparing Expected Value and Expected Utility
text_blocks: Comparing Expected Value and Expected Utility

Expected Utility
• A subjective measure
• Reflects an individual's personal satisfaction and
risk tolerance
• Allows individuals to make decisions that align with
their personal risk aversion and preferences
• Can vary significantly from person to person
• Incorporates personal judgments and feelings about
risk and reward

Expected Value
• An objective measure
• Remains constant regardless of who is evaluating it
• Calculated based on probabilities and outcomes
• Provides a consistent metric
• Does not change with individual perceptions or
preferences

In summary, while expected value offers a stable, objective assessment, expected utility provides a personalized approach,
incorporating individual risk preferences and satisfaction levels. Now, how can leaders effectively deliver value in public
health?
